14: 
15: # Deep Review Quick Reference
16: 
17: One-page cheat sheet for the autonomous deep review loop.
18: 
19: ---
20: 
21: ## 1. OVERVIEW
22: 
23: ### Purpose
24: 
25: Use this quick reference when you need the command shape, artifact names, convergence rules, and synthesis outputs for the autonomous deep-review loop without loading the full protocol references.
26: 
27: ### When to Use
28: 
29: - Looking up the canonical `/deep:review` invocation without re-reading the full SKILL.md.
30: - Confirming default thresholds, verdicts, or release-readiness states at a glance.
31: - Refreshing the artifact-name and packet-file checklist before a manual orchestration pass.
32: - Triaging a stuck loop and need the fastest path to convergence semantics.
33: 
34: ### Commands
35: 
36: | Command | Description |
37: |---------|-------------|
38: | `/deep:review:auto "target"` | Run autonomous review (no approval gates) |
39: | `/deep:review:confirm "target"` | Run review with approval gates at each iteration |
40: | `/deep:review "target"` | Ask which mode to use |
41: 
42: ### Parameters
43: 
44: | Parameter | Default | Description |
45: |-----------|---------|-------------|
46: | `--max-iterations` | 7 | Maximum review iterations |
47: | `--convergence` | 0.10 | Base sensitivity for review convergence |
48: | `--stop-policy` | convergence | Use `max-iterations` when convergence must not end the run before the iteration ceiling |
49: | `--spec-folder` | auto | Target spec folder path |
50: | `--restart` | off | Archive the current review packet and start a fresh lineage |
51: | `--severity-threshold` | P2 | Minimum severity to report |
52: 
53: ---
54: 
55: ## 2. WHEN TO USE
56: 
57: | Scenario | Use |
58: |----------|-----|
59: | Multi-pass code quality audit | `/deep:review` |
60: | Simple single-pass code review | `sk-code-review` |
61: | Pre-release readiness check | `/deep:review:auto "spec folder"` |
62: | Spec/implementation alignment check | `/deep:review:auto "skill sk-name"` |
63: | Deep technical investigation | `/deep:research` (different skill) |
64: 
65: ---
66: 
67: ## 3. ARCHITECTURE
68: 
69: ```
70: /deep:review  -->  YAML workflow  -->  @deep-review agent (LEAF)
71:     |                    |                      |
72:     |                    |                      +-- Read state
73:     |                    |                      +-- Review (3-5 actions)
74:     |                    |                      +-- Write findings (P0/P1/P2)
75:     |                    |                      +-- Update state
76:     |                    |
77:     |                    +-- Init (config, strategy, state)
78:     |                    +-- Loop (dispatch, evaluate, decide)
79:     |                    +-- Synthesize (review-report.md)
80:     |                    +-- Save (memory context)
81: ```
82: 
83: ---
84: 
85: ## 4. STATE FILES
86: 
87: Review mode stores its packet under the resolved local review path rooted at `{spec_folder}/review/`:
88: 
89: | File | Location | Format | Purpose |
90: |------|----------|--------|---------|
91: | Config | `review/deep-review-config.json` | JSON | Review parameters (immutable) |
92: | State | `review/deep-review-state.jsonl` | JSONL | Iteration log (append-only) |
93: | Registry | `review/deep-review-findings-registry.json` | JSON | Reducer-owned finding registry |
94: | Strategy | `review/deep-review-strategy.md` | Markdown | Dimensions, findings, next focus |
95: | Dashboard | `review/deep-review-dashboard.md` | Markdown | Auto-generated review dashboard |
96: | Iterations | `review/iterations/iteration-NNN.md` | Markdown | Per-iteration findings (write-once) |
97: | Report | `review/review-report.md` | Markdown | Final 9-section review report |
98: | Pause | `review/.deep-review-pause` | Sentinel | Pause between iterations |
99: 
100: ### Lifecycle Modes
101: 
102: | Mode | Effect |
103: |------|--------|
104: | `resume` | Continue the current review lineage without resetting generation |
105: | `restart` | Archive current review state and start a new generation |
106: | `fork` (deferred) | Reserved. Start a sibling lineage branch with explicit parent linkage. Not runtime-wired. |
107: | `completed-continue` (deferred) | Reserved. Snapshot the completed review and reopen it for amendment-only review deltas. Not runtime-wired. |
108: 
109: ---
110: 
111: ## 5. REVIEW DIMENSIONS
112: 
113: | ID | Dimension | Priority | Description |
114: |----|-----------|----------|-------------|
115: | D1 | Correctness | 1 | Logic errors, off-by-one, wrong return types, broken invariants |
116: | D2 | Security | 2 | Injection, auth bypass, secrets exposure, unsafe deserialization |
117: | D3 | Traceability | 3 | Spec/code alignment, checklist evidence, cross-reference integrity |
118: | D4 | Maintainability | 4 | Patterns, clarity, documentation quality, ease of safe follow-on changes |
119: 
120: ---
121: 
122: ## 6. REVIEW VERDICTS
123: 
124: | Verdict | Condition | Meaning | Next Command |
125: |---------|-----------|---------|--------------|
126: | FAIL | Active P0 findings remain OR any binary gate fails | Does not meet quality standards | `/speckit:plan` for remediation |
127: | CONDITIONAL | No P0, but active P1 findings remain | Meets threshold but has required fixes | `/speckit:plan` for fixes |
128: | PASS | No active P0/P1 findings | Shippable, set `hasAdvisories=true` when P2 findings remain | `/create:changelog` |
129: 
130: ### Release Readiness
131: 
132: `releaseReadinessState` is the canonical config/report field for review readiness tracking.
133: 
134: | State | Meaning |
135: |-------|---------|
136: | `in-progress` | Review still running or required coverage incomplete |
137: | `converged` | All 4 dimensions covered and the stabilization pass found no new P0/P1 findings |
138: | `release-blocking` | At least one unresolved P0 remains active |
139: 
140: ---
141: 
142: ## 7. REVIEW QUALITY GUARDS
143: 
144: | Gate | Rule |
145: |------|------|
146: | Evidence | Every active finding has file:line evidence and is not inference-only |
147: | Scope | Findings and reviewed files stay within declared review scope |
148: | Coverage | Configured dimensions plus required traceability protocols are covered before STOP |
149: 
150: ---
151: 
152: ## 8. REVIEW CONVERGENCE
153: 
154: | Signal | Weight | Description |
155: |--------|--------|-------------|
156: | Rolling Average | 0.30 | Last 2 severity-weighted `newFindingsRatio` values average below `0.08` |
157: | MAD Noise Floor | 0.25 | Latest ratio within noise floor |
158: | Dimension Coverage | 0.45 | All 4 dimensions plus required traceability protocols covered, with `minStabilizationPasses >= 1` |
159: 
160: **Key defaults:** `maxIterations=7`, `convergenceThreshold=0.10`, `rollingStopThreshold=0.08`, `noProgressThreshold=0.05`, `stuckThreshold=2`, `minStabilizationPasses=1`
161: 
162: **P0 override:** Any new P0 finding sets `newFindingsRatio >= 0.50`, blocking convergence.
163: 
164: ---
165: 
166: ## 9. AGENT ITERATION CHECKLIST
167: 
168: Each @deep-review iteration:
169: 1. Read `deep-review-state.jsonl`, `deep-review-findings-registry.json`, and `deep-review-strategy.md`
170: 2. Determine focus dimension from strategy "Next Focus"
171: 3. Execute 3-5 review actions (Read, Grep, Glob, code_graph_query + Grep)
172: 4. Write `review/iterations/iteration-NNN.md` with P0/P1/P2 findings
173: 5. Run adversarial self-check on any P0 findings (Hunter/Skeptic/Referee)
174: 6. Update `deep-review-strategy.md` (findings, coverage, next focus)
175: 7. Append iteration record to `deep-review-state.jsonl`
176: 
177: ---
178: 
179: ## 10. REVIEW REPORT SECTIONS
180: 
181: | # | Section | Purpose |
182: |---|---------|---------|
183: | 1 | Executive Summary | Verdict, active P0/P1/P2 counts, scope, `hasAdvisories` |
184: | 2 | Planning Trigger | Why the verdict routes to planning or changelog follow-up |
185: | 3 | Active Finding Registry | Deduped active findings with evidence and final severity |
186: | 4 | Remediation Workstreams | Grouped action lanes derived from active findings |
187: | 5 | Spec Seed | Minimal spec delta derived from review results |
188: | 6 | Plan Seed | Action-ready plan starter for remediation |
189: | 7 | Traceability Status | Core vs overlay protocol status and unresolved gaps |
190: | 8 | Deferred Items | P2 advisories, blocked checks, and follow-up items |
191: | 9 | Audit Appendix | Coverage, replay validation, and convergence evidence |
192: 
193: ---
194: 
195: ## 11. TUNING GUIDE
196: 
197: | Goal | Adjustment |
198: |------|------------|
199: | Deeper review | Lower convergence (0.05), raise max iterations (10) |
200: | Forced iteration ceiling | Set `--stop-policy=max-iterations` with the desired `--max-iterations` |
201: | Faster completion | Raise convergence (0.15), lower max iterations (5) |
202: | Focus on security | Specify `--dimensions security,correctness` |
203: | Broad coverage | Use all 4 default dimensions and allow the stabilization pass to run |
204: 
205: ---
206: 
207: ## 12. TROUBLESHOOTING
208: 
209: | Problem | Fix |
210: |---------|-----|
211: | Stops too early | Lower `--convergence` from 0.10 to 0.05, or use `--stop-policy=max-iterations` when early convergence is unacceptable |
212: | Stuck on one dimension | Check strategy.md "Next Focus" rotation |
213: | P0 findings blocking convergence | Expected behavior: P0 override prevents premature stop |
214: | Review-report missing sections | Verify synthesis phase completed (check state.jsonl for synthesis event) |
215: | Agent ignores state | Verify file paths in dispatch prompt |
216: 
217: ---
218: 
219: ## 13. RELATED
220: 
221: | Resource | Purpose |
222: |----------|---------|
223: | `deep-research` | Investigation/research (not review) |
224: | `sk-code-review` | Single-pass code review |
225: | `@deep-review` | Single review iteration agent (LEAF) |
226: | `@context` | Single-pass codebase search |
227: | `generate-context.js` | Memory save script |
228: 
229: For review-specific protocol documentation, see local `references/`:
230: - `loop_protocol.md`, Review loop lifecycle (4 phases)
231: - `../state/state_format.md`, Review state file schemas
232: - `../convergence/convergence.md`, Review convergence algorithms

# Deep Review Quick Reference

One-page cheat sheet for the autonomous deep review loop.

---

## 1. OVERVIEW

### Purpose

Use this quick reference when you need the command shape, artifact names, convergence rules, and synthesis outputs for the autonomous deep-review loop without loading the full protocol references.

### When to Use

- Looking up the canonical `/deep:review` invocation without re-reading the full SKILL.md.
- Confirming default thresholds, verdicts, or release-readiness states at a glance.
- Refreshing the artifact-name and packet-file checklist before a manual orchestration pass.
- Triaging a stuck loop and need the fastest path to convergence semantics.

### Commands

| Command | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| `/deep:review:auto "target"` | Run autonomous review (no approval gates) |
| `/deep:review:confirm "target"` | Run review with approval gates at each iteration |
| `/deep:review "target"` | Ask which mode to use |

### Parameters

| Parameter | Default | Description |
|-----------|---------|-------------|
| `--max-iterations` | 7 | Maximum review iterations |
| `--convergence` | 0.10 | Base sensitivity for review convergence |
| `--stop-policy` | convergence | Use `max-iterations` when convergence must not end the run before the iteration ceiling |
| `--spec-folder` | auto | Target spec folder path |
| `--restart` | off | Archive the current review packet and start a fresh lineage |
| `--severity-threshold` | P2 | Minimum severity to report |

---

## 2. WHEN TO USE

| Scenario | Use |
|----------|-----|
| Multi-pass code quality audit | `/deep:review` |
| Simple single-pass code review | `sk-code-review` |
| Pre-release readiness check | `/deep:review:auto "spec folder"` |
| Spec/implementation alignment check | `/deep:review:auto "skill sk-name"` |
| Deep technical investigation | `/deep:research` (different skill) |

---

## 3. ARCHITECTURE

```
/deep:review  -->  YAML workflow  -->  @deep-review agent (LEAF)
    |                    |                      |
    |                    |                      +-- Read state
    |                    |                      +-- Review (3-5 actions)
    |                    |                      +-- Write findings (P0/P1/P2)
    |                    |                      +-- Update state
    |                    |
    |                    +-- Init (config, strategy, state)
    |                    +-- Loop (dispatch, evaluate, decide)
    |                    +-- Synthesize (review-report.md)
    |                    +-- Save (memory context)
```

---

## 4. STATE FILES

Review mode stores its packet under the resolved local review path rooted at `{spec_folder}/review/`:

| File | Location | Format | Purpose |
|------|----------|--------|---------|
| Config | `review/deep-review-config.json` | JSON | Review parameters (immutable) |
| State | `review/deep-review-state.jsonl` | JSONL | Iteration log (append-only) |
| Registry | `review/deep-review-findings-registry.json` | JSON | Reducer-owned finding registry |
| Strategy | `review/deep-review-strategy.md` | Markdown | Dimensions, findings, next focus |
| Dashboard | `review/deep-review-dashboard.md` | Markdown | Auto-generated review dashboard |
| Iterations | `review/iterations/iteration-NNN.md` | Markdown | Per-iteration findings (write-once) |
| Report | `review/review-report.md` | Markdown | Final 9-section review report |
| Pause | `review/.deep-review-pause` | Sentinel | Pause between iterations |

### Lifecycle Modes

| Mode | Effect |
|------|--------|
| `resume` | Continue the current review lineage without resetting generation |
| `restart` | Archive current review state and start a new generation |
| `fork` (deferred) | Reserved. Start a sibling lineage branch with explicit parent linkage. Not runtime-wired. |
| `completed-continue` (deferred) | Reserved. Snapshot the completed review and reopen it for amendment-only review deltas. Not runtime-wired. |

---

## 5. REVIEW DIMENSIONS

| ID | Dimension | Priority | Description |
|----|-----------|----------|-------------|
| D1 | Correctness | 1 | Logic errors, off-by-one, wrong return types, broken invariants |
| D2 | Security | 2 | Injection, auth bypass, secrets exposure, unsafe deserialization |
| D3 | Traceability | 3 | Spec/code alignment, checklist evidence, cross-reference integrity |
| D4 | Maintainability | 4 | Patterns, clarity, documentation quality, ease of safe follow-on changes |

---

## 6. REVIEW VERDICTS

| Verdict | Condition | Meaning | Next Command |
|---------|-----------|---------|--------------|
| FAIL | Active P0 findings remain OR any binary gate fails | Does not meet quality standards | `/speckit:plan` for remediation |
| CONDITIONAL | No P0, but active P1 findings remain | Meets threshold but has required fixes | `/speckit:plan` for fixes |
| PASS | No active P0/P1 findings | Shippable, set `hasAdvisories=true` when P2 findings remain | `/create:changelog` |

### Release Readiness

`releaseReadinessState` is the canonical config/report field for review readiness tracking.

| State | Meaning |
|-------|---------|
| `in-progress` | Review still running or required coverage incomplete |
| `converged` | All 4 dimensions covered and the stabilization pass found no new P0/P1 findings |
| `release-blocking` | At least one unresolved P0 remains active |

---

## 7. REVIEW QUALITY GUARDS

| Gate | Rule |
|------|------|
| Evidence | Every active finding has file:line evidence and is not inference-only |
| Scope | Findings and reviewed files stay within declared review scope |
| Coverage | Configured dimensions plus required traceability protocols are covered before STOP |

---

## 8. REVIEW CONVERGENCE

| Signal | Weight | Description |
|--------|--------|-------------|
| Rolling Average | 0.30 | Last 2 severity-weighted `newFindingsRatio` values average below `0.08` |
| MAD Noise Floor | 0.25 | Latest ratio within noise floor |
| Dimension Coverage | 0.45 | All 4 dimensions plus required traceability protocols covered, with `minStabilizationPasses >= 1` |

**Key defaults:** `maxIterations=7`, `convergenceThreshold=0.10`, `rollingStopThreshold=0.08`, `noProgressThreshold=0.05`, `stuckThreshold=2`, `minStabilizationPasses=1`

**P0 override:** Any new P0 finding sets `newFindingsRatio >= 0.50`, blocking convergence.

---

## 9. AGENT ITERATION CHECKLIST

Each @deep-review iteration:
1. Read `deep-review-state.jsonl`, `deep-review-findings-registry.json`, and `deep-review-strategy.md`
2. Determine focus dimension from strategy "Next Focus"
3. Execute 3-5 review actions (Read, Grep, Glob, code_graph_query + Grep)
4. Write `review/iterations/iteration-NNN.md` with P0/P1/P2 findings
5. Run adversarial self-check on any P0 findings (Hunter/Skeptic/Referee)
6. Update `deep-review-strategy.md` (findings, coverage, next focus)
7. Append iteration record to `deep-review-state.jsonl`

---

## 10. REVIEW REPORT SECTIONS

| # | Section | Purpose |
|---|---------|---------|
| 1 | Executive Summary | Verdict, active P0/P1/P2 counts, scope, `hasAdvisories` |
| 2 | Planning Trigger | Why the verdict routes to planning or changelog follow-up |
| 3 | Active Finding Registry | Deduped active findings with evidence and final severity |
| 4 | Remediation Workstreams | Grouped action lanes derived from active findings |
| 5 | Spec Seed | Minimal spec delta derived from review results |
| 6 | Plan Seed | Action-ready plan starter for remediation |
| 7 | Traceability Status | Core vs overlay protocol status and unresolved gaps |
| 8 | Deferred Items | P2 advisories, blocked checks, and follow-up items |
| 9 | Audit Appendix | Coverage, replay validation, and convergence evidence |

---

## 11. TUNING GUIDE

| Goal | Adjustment |
|------|------------|
| Deeper review | Lower convergence (0.05), raise max iterations (10) |
| Forced iteration ceiling | Set `--stop-policy=max-iterations` with the desired `--max-iterations` |
| Faster completion | Raise convergence (0.15), lower max iterations (5) |
| Focus on security | Specify `--dimensions security,correctness` |
| Broad coverage | Use all 4 default dimensions and allow the stabilization pass to run |

---

## 12. TROUBLESHOOTING

| Problem | Fix |
|---------|-----|
| Stops too early | Lower `--convergence` from 0.10 to 0.05, or use `--stop-policy=max-iterations` when early convergence is unacceptable |
| Stuck on one dimension | Check strategy.md "Next Focus" rotation |
| P0 findings blocking convergence | Expected behavior: P0 override prevents premature stop |
| Review-report missing sections | Verify synthesis phase completed (check state.jsonl for synthesis event) |
| Agent ignores state | Verify file paths in dispatch prompt |

---

## 13. RELATED

| Resource | Purpose |
|----------|---------|
| `deep-research` | Investigation/research (not review) |
| `sk-code-review` | Single-pass code review |
| `@deep-review` | Single review iteration agent (LEAF) |
| `@context` | Single-pass codebase search |
| `generate-context.js` | Memory save script |

For review-specific protocol documentation, see local `references/`:

13: ---
14: 
15: # Deep Review State Format
16: 
17: State file schemas for the autonomous deep review loop.
18: 
19: ---
20: 
21: ## 1. OVERVIEW
22: 
23: The deep review loop uses 8 state files under the resolved `{artifact_dir}/` owned by the target spec:
24: 
25: | File | Format | Mutability |
26: |------|--------|------------|
27: | `deep-review-config.json` | JSON | Immutable after init |
28: | `deep-review-state.jsonl` | JSON Lines | Append-only |
29: | `deep-review-findings-registry.json` | JSON | Auto-generated reducer state |
30: | `deep-review-strategy.md` | Markdown | Updated each iteration |
31: | `deep-review-dashboard.md` | Markdown | Auto-generated (read-only) |
32: | `.deep-review-pause` | Sentinel | Created/deleted by user |
33: | `review-report.md` | Markdown | Updated at synthesis |
34: | `iterations/iteration-NNN.md` | Markdown | Write-once |
35: 
36: ```text
37: {artifact_dir}/
38:   deep-review-config.json
39:   deep-review-state.jsonl
40:   deep-review-findings-registry.json
41:   deep-review-strategy.md
42:   deep-review-dashboard.md
43:   .deep-review-pause
44:   review-report.md
45:   iterations/
46:     iteration-001.md
47:     iteration-002.md
48:     ...
49: ```
50: 
51: `{artifact_dir}` comes from `resolveArtifactRoot(specFolder, 'review')`. Root-spec runs resolve directly to `{spec_folder}/review/`. Child-phase and sub-phase runs use **flat-first**: a first run with an empty `review/` resolves directly to `{spec_folder}/review/` (no `pt-NN` wrapper). A `pt-NN` packet is allocated only when prior content for a non-matching target already exists. Continuation runs reuse the existing flat artifact (or matching `pt-NN` packet).
52: 
53: ### When to Use
54: 
55: - Validating deep-review state files emitted by an iteration or by the reducer.
56: - Implementing a state consumer (e.g. dashboard, advisor probe, downstream packet).
57: - Debugging JSONL reducer output or auditing schema-version transitions.
58: - Authoring fixtures that need to round-trip through the reducer.
59: 
60: ---
61: 
62: ## 2. CONFIG FILE (deep-review-config.json)
63: 
64: Created during initialization. Not modified after creation.
65: 
66: ```json
67: {
68:   "topic": "Review of deep-review skill package",
69:   "mode": "review",
70:   "reviewTarget": "specs/030-deep-review-review-mode",

119: ### Field Reference
120: 
121: | Field | Type | Default | Description |
122: |-------|------|---------|-------------|
123: | mode | `"review"` | -- | Session mode discriminator (required) |
124: | reviewTarget | string | -- | Path or identifier of the review target |

129: | parentSessionId | string \| null | `null` | Parent lineage reference for restart flows |

131: | generation | number | 1 | Lineage generation number, incremented on `restart`, unchanged on `resume` |
132: | continuedFromRun | number \| null | `null` | Count of completed iteration records at the lifecycle boundary (set on `resume` and `restart`) |

135: | stuckThreshold | number | 2 | Consecutive no-progress iterations before recovery |
136: | severityThreshold | string | `"P2"` | Minimum severity to report: `P0`, `P1`, `P2` |
137: | crossReference | object | -- | Core (hard-gated) and overlay (advisory) protocol sets |
138: | qualityGateThreshold | boolean | true | Whether binary quality gates are enforced |
139: | specFolder | string | -- | Spec folder path (relative to specs/) |
140: | status | string | `"initialized"` | `initialized`, `running`, `converged`, `stuck`, `complete`, `error` |
141: | releaseReadinessState | string | `"in-progress"` | `in-progress`, `converged`, `release-blocking` |
142: | fileProtection | object | -- | Mutability declarations (see protection levels below) |
143: | reducer | object | -- | Canonical reducer inputs, outputs, and metrics names |

145: ### File Protection Levels

147: | Level | Meaning |

149: | immutable | Cannot be modified after creation |

175: ## 3. STATE LOG
176: 
177: The `deep-review-state.jsonl` record schemas (config, iteration, event, lineage) and the Review Depth Schema (v2) live in `state_jsonl.md` for navigability.

181: ## 4. STRATEGY FILE (deep-review-strategy.md)
182: 
183: Updated at the end of each iteration. Serves as the persistent brain across fresh-context iterations.
184: 
185: ### Required Sections
186: 
187: | Section | Updated By | Purpose |
188: |---------|-----------|---------|
189: | topic | Init only | Review topic from config |
190: | review-dimensions | Each iteration | Unchecked dimensions drive next focus |
191: | completed-dimensions | Each iteration | Checked dimensions with verdict summary |
192: | running-findings | Each iteration | P0/P1/P2 active counts + deltas |
193: | what-worked | Each iteration | Successful approaches for reuse |
194: | what-failed | Each iteration | Failed approaches to avoid |
195: | exhausted-approaches | When fully tried | "Do not retry" registry |
196: | ruled-out-directions | Each iteration | Eliminated directions with reasons |
197: | next-focus | Each iteration | Recommended direction for next iteration |
198: | known-context | Init only | Prior work from memory context |
199: | cross-reference-status | Each iteration | Core vs overlay protocol status |
200: | files-under-review | Each iteration | Per-file coverage state table |
201: | review-boundaries | Init only | Max iterations, thresholds, config |
202: 
203: ### Mapping from Research Mode
204: 
205: | Review Section | Research Equivalent |
206: |---------------|-------------------|
207: | review-dimensions | Key Questions (remaining) |
208: | completed-dimensions | Answered Questions |
209: | running-findings | _(none, review-specific)_ |
210: | cross-reference-status | _(none, review-specific)_ |
211: | files-under-review | _(none, review-specific)_ |
212: | review-boundaries | Research Boundaries |
213: 
214: Sections unchanged from research: topic, what-worked, what-failed, exhausted-approaches, ruled-out-directions, next-focus, known-context.
215: 
216: ### Update Protocol
217: 
218: 1. Read current strategy file
219: 2. Move completed dimensions from "remaining" to "completed" with `[x]` and verdict
220: 3. Update running-findings with P0/P1/P2 counts and deltas
221: 4. Add to what-worked / what-failed with iteration number
222: 5. Move exhausted approaches. Update cross-reference-status and files-under-review
223: 6. Set next-focus based on remaining dimensions and open findings
224: 
225: ---
226: 
227: ## 5. FINDINGS REGISTRY (deep-review-findings-registry.json)
228: 
229: Reducer-owned JSON document regenerated after every iteration and lifecycle transition.
230: 
231: ```json
232: {
233:   "sessionId": "rvw-2026-04-03T12-00-00Z",
234:   "generation": 1,
235:   "lineageMode": "resume",
236:   "openFindings": [],
237:   "resolvedFindings": [],
238:   "blockedStopHistory": [],
239:   "persistentSameSeverity": [],
240:   "severityChanged": [],
241:   "repeatedFindings": [],
242:   "dimensionCoverage": {
243:     "correctness": true,
244:     "security": true,
245:     "traceability": false,
246:     "maintainability": false
247:   },
248:   "findingsBySeverity": {
249:     "P0": 0,
250:     "P1": 1,
251:     "P2": 2
252:   },
253:   "openFindingsCount": 3,
254:   "resolvedFindingsCount": 1,
255:   "convergenceScore": 0.44,
256:   "graphConvergenceScore": 1.01,
257:   "graphDecision": "STOP_ALLOWED",
258:   "graphBlockers": [],
259:   "corruptionWarnings": []
260: }
261: ```
262: 
263: This file is machine-owned and must stay synchronized with the latest iteration delta, dashboard metrics, and synthesized review report.
264: 
265: ### Blocked-Stop and Corruption-Tracking Additions
266: 
267: | Field | Type | Description |
268: |-------|------|-------------|
269: | `blockedStopHistory` | array | One entry per `blocked_stop` JSONL event: `{run, blockedBy, gateResults, recoveryStrategy, timestamp}`. Rendered in the dashboard `BLOCKED STOPS` section and can drive the strategy `next-focus` anchor when blocked-stop is the most recent loop event. |
270: | `persistentSameSeverity` | array | Findings observed in ≥2 iterations with NO severity transitions beyond initial discovery. REQ-018 split of the deprecated `repeatedFindings` bucket. |
271: | `severityChanged` | array | Findings that went through at least one severity transition (P0↔P1↔P2) in their `transitions` history. |
272: | `repeatedFindings` | array | **Deprecated.** Union of `persistentSameSeverity` and `severityChanged`. Retained for backward compatibility, new code should read the split arrays. |
273: | `corruptionWarnings` | array | Per-line corruption reports from `parseJsonlDetailed()`: `{line, raw, error}`. Non-empty means the reducer detected malformed JSONL. |
274: 
275: ### Default Values
276: 
277: When no `graph_convergence` event has been recorded yet, defaults are `graphConvergenceScore: 0`, `graphDecision: null`, and `graphBlockers: []`.
278: When no `blocked_stop` event has been recorded yet, `blockedStopHistory: []`.
279: When JSONL parses cleanly, `corruptionWarnings: []`.
280: 
281: ### Fail-Closed Semantics (REQ-015, REQ-016)
282: 
283: - **Malformed JSONL**: The reducer CLI exits with code `2` and writes a warning to stderr when `corruptionWarnings.length > 0`. Pass `--lenient` (or `lenient:true` to `reduceReviewState`) to escape-hatch out and preserve the v1.2.0.0 fail-open behavior for legacy packets.
284: - **Missing machine-owned anchors**: `replaceAnchorSection()` throws `Error('Missing machine-owned anchor "<id>" in deep-review strategy file.')` when the strategy file is present but lacks one of the required anchors. Pass `--create-missing-anchors` (or `createMissingAnchors:true`) to bootstrap empty strategy files by appending the missing anchor blocks.
285: - **Dashboard surfaces**: `CORRUPTION WARNINGS` section lists detected lines. `BLOCKED STOPS` section lists `blockedStopHistory` entries. `GRAPH CONVERGENCE` section reports `graphConvergenceScore` / `graphDecision` / `graphBlockers`.
286: - **Strategy next-focus override**: When the latest `blocked_stop` event timestamp is newer than the latest iteration timestamp, the reducer rewrites the strategy `next-focus` anchor to surface the blocking gates and recovery hint so operators see the blocker before choosing the next iteration direction.
287: 
288: ---
289: 
290: ## 6. ITERATION FILES (review/iterations/iteration-NNN.md)
291: 
292: Write-once files. One per iteration, zero-padded 3-digit naming.
293: 
294: ### Structure
295: 
296: ```markdown
297: # Iteration [N]: [Focus Area]
298: 
299: ## Focus
300: [Dimension(s), files, scope investigated]
301: 
302: ## Scorecard
303: - Dimensions covered: [list]
304: - Files reviewed: [count]
305: - New findings: P0=[n] P1=[n] P2=[n]
306: - Refined findings: P0=[n] P1=[n] P2=[n]
307: - New findings ratio: [0.0-1.0]
308: 
309: ## Findings
310: 
311: ### P0, Blocker
312: - **F[NNN]**: [Title], `file:line`, [Description with evidence]
313: 
314: ### P1, Required
315: - **F[NNN]**: [Title], `file:line`, [Description with evidence]
316: 
317: ### P2, Suggestion
318: - **F[NNN]**: [Title], `file:line`, [Description with evidence]
319: 
320: ## Cross-Reference Results
321: | Protocol | Status | Gate | Evidence | Notes |
322: |----------|--------|------|----------|-------|
323: | spec_code | pass/partial/fail | hard | file:line | ... |
324: 
325: ## Assessment
326: - New findings ratio: [0.0-1.0]
327: - Dimensions addressed: [list]
328: - Novelty justification: [breakdown]
329: 
330: ## Ruled Out
331: - [Approach]: [Why], [Evidence]
332: 
333: ## Dead Ends
334: - [Direction]: [Why]
335: 
336: ## Recommended Next Focus
337: [What to investigate next]
338: ```
339: 
340: Every finding must include: unique ID (`F001`...), severity (`P0`/`P1`/`P2`), concrete `file:line` evidence, and dimension tag.
341: 
342: ---
343: 
344: ## 7. REVIEW REPORT (review/review-report.md)
345: 
346: The review synthesis output contains 9 core sections plus a conditional `## Resource Map Coverage Gate` section when `resource_map_present` is true:
347: 
348: | # | Section | Description |
349: |---|---------|-------------|
350: | 1 | Executive Summary | Verdict, active P0/P1/P2 counts, scope, `hasAdvisories` flag |
351: | 2 | Planning Trigger | Why result routes to planning or changelog |
352: | 3 | Active Finding Registry | Deduped active findings with evidence, severity, dimension |
353: | 4 | Remediation Workstreams | Ordered lanes grouped by dependency or area |
354: | 5 | Spec Seed | Minimal spec updates implied by findings |
355: | 6 | Plan Seed | Initial remediation tasks from findings |
356: | 7 | Traceability Status | Core vs overlay protocol outcomes and gaps |
357: | 8 | Resource Map Coverage Gate | Present only when `{spec_folder}/resource-map.md` existed at init, contains touched entries, untouched entries (`expected-by-scope` vs `gap`), and implementation paths absent from the map |
358: | 9 | Deferred Items | Advisory findings, blocked items, follow-up checks |
359: | 10 | Audit Appendix | Coverage, convergence replay, audit detail |
360: 
361: **Executive Summary** includes verdict (`PASS`/`CONDITIONAL`/`FAIL`), active finding counts, `hasAdvisories` boolean (PASS + P2 > 0), scope description, and convergence reason.
362: 
363: **Planning Trigger** routes FAIL/CONDITIONAL to remediation planning, PASS to changelog creation.
364: 
365: **Active Finding Registry** lists each active finding with findingId, severity, dimension, title, file:line evidence, first/last seen iteration, and status.
366: 
367: **Remediation Workstreams** group related findings into ordered lanes with constituent finding IDs and execution order.
368: 
369: **Spec Seed / Plan Seed** provide minimal spec updates and initial remediation tasks referencing finding IDs and target files.
370: 
371: **Traceability Status** reports per-protocol pass/partial/fail with gating class and evidence.
372: 
373: **Resource Map Coverage Gate** is emitted only when `resource_map_present` is true and MUST contain exactly three bullets: touched entries, untouched entries (`expected-by-scope` vs `gap`), and implementation paths absent from the map.
374: 
375: **Deferred Items** captures advisory findings, blocked protocols, and future follow-up checks.
376: 
377: **Audit Appendix** contains iteration table, convergence signal replay, file coverage matrix, and dimension breakdown.
378: 
379: ---
380: 
381: ## 8. DASHBOARD (review/deep-review-dashboard.md)
382: 
383: Auto-generated summary. Never manually edited.
384: 
385: - **Path**: `{artifact_dir}/deep-review-dashboard.md`
386: - **Generated from**: JSONL state log + strategy + findings registry
387: - **Refresh**: Regenerated after every iteration
388: - **Protection**: `auto-generated` in fileProtection
389: 
390: ### Sections
391: 
392: | Section | Source | Description |
393: |---------|--------|-------------|
394: | Findings Summary | JSONL | Active P0/P1/P2 counts, new vs refined |
395: | Progress Table | JSONL | Run, status, focus, dimensions, ratio, duration |
396: | Coverage | Strategy + JSONL | Dimension completion, file coverage, protocol status |
397: | Trend | JSONL signals | Rolling average, composite stop score, trajectory |
398: 
399: **Rules:** Sole inputs are JSONL + strategy. Overwrite entirely on refresh. Read-only for all agents.

# Deep Review State Format

State file schemas for the autonomous deep review loop.

---

## 1. OVERVIEW

The deep review loop uses 8 state files under the resolved `{artifact_dir}/` owned by the target spec:

| File | Format | Mutability |
|------|--------|------------|
| `deep-review-config.json` | JSON | Immutable after init |
| `deep-review-state.jsonl` | JSON Lines | Append-only |
| `deep-review-findings-registry.json` | JSON | Auto-generated reducer state |
| `deep-review-strategy.md` | Markdown | Updated each iteration |
| `deep-review-dashboard.md` | Markdown | Auto-generated (read-only) |
| `.deep-review-pause` | Sentinel | Created/deleted by user |
| `review-report.md` | Markdown | Updated at synthesis |
| `iterations/iteration-NNN.md` | Markdown | Write-once |

```text
{artifact_dir}/
  deep-review-config.json
  deep-review-state.jsonl
  deep-review-findings-registry.json
  deep-review-strategy.md
  deep-review-dashboard.md
  .deep-review-pause
  review-report.md
  iterations/
    iteration-001.md
    iteration-002.md
    ...
```

`{artifact_dir}` comes from `resolveArtifactRoot(specFolder, 'review')`. Root-spec runs resolve directly to `{spec_folder}/review/`. Child-phase and sub-phase runs use **flat-first**: a first run with an empty `review/` resolves directly to `{spec_folder}/review/` (no `pt-NN` wrapper). A `pt-NN` packet is allocated only when prior content for a non-matching target already exists. Continuation runs reuse the existing flat artifact (or matching `pt-NN` packet).

### When to Use

- Validating deep-review state files emitted by an iteration or by the reducer.
- Implementing a state consumer (e.g. dashboard, advisor probe, downstream packet).
- Debugging JSONL reducer output or auditing schema-version transitions.
- Authoring fixtures that need to round-trip through the reducer.

---

### File Protection Levels

| immutable | Cannot be modified after creation |

### Review Dimensions

## 4. STRATEGY FILE (deep-review-strategy.md)

Updated at the end of each iteration. Serves as the persistent brain across fresh-context iterations.

### Required Sections

| Section | Updated By | Purpose |
|---------|-----------|---------|
| topic | Init only | Review topic from config |
| review-dimensions | Each iteration | Unchecked dimensions drive next focus |
| completed-dimensions | Each iteration | Checked dimensions with verdict summary |
| running-findings | Each iteration | P0/P1/P2 active counts + deltas |
| what-worked | Each iteration | Successful approaches for reuse |
| what-failed | Each iteration | Failed approaches to avoid |
| exhausted-approaches | When fully tried | "Do not retry" registry |
| ruled-out-directions | Each iteration | Eliminated directions with reasons |
| next-focus | Each iteration | Recommended direction for next iteration |
| known-context | Init only | Prior work from memory context |
| cross-reference-status | Each iteration | Core vs overlay protocol status |
| files-under-review | Each iteration | Per-file coverage state table |
| review-boundaries | Init only | Max iterations, thresholds, config |

### Mapping from Research Mode

| Review Section | Research Equivalent |
|---------------|-------------------|
| review-dimensions | Key Questions (remaining) |
| completed-dimensions | Answered Questions |
| running-findings | _(none, review-specific)_ |
| cross-reference-status | _(none, review-specific)_ |
| files-under-review | _(none, review-specific)_ |
| review-boundaries | Research Boundaries |

Sections unchanged from research: topic, what-worked, what-failed, exhausted-approaches, ruled-out-directions, next-focus, known-context.

### Update Protocol

1. Read current strategy file
2. Move completed dimensions from "remaining" to "completed" with `[x]` and verdict
3. Update running-findings with P0/P1/P2 counts and deltas
4. Add to what-worked / what-failed with iteration number
5. Move exhausted approaches. Update cross-reference-status and files-under-review
6. Set next-focus based on remaining dimensions and open findings

---

## 5. FINDINGS REGISTRY (deep-review-findings-registry.json)

Reducer-owned JSON document regenerated after every iteration and lifecycle transition.

```json
{
  "sessionId": "rvw-2026-04-03T12-00-00Z",
  "generation": 1,
  "lineageMode": "resume",
  "openFindings": [],
  "resolvedFindings": [],
  "blockedStopHistory": [],
  "persistentSameSeverity": [],
  "severityChanged": [],
  "repeatedFindings": [],
  "dimensionCoverage": {
    "correctness": true,
    "security": true,
    "traceability": false,
    "maintainability": false
  },
  "findingsBySeverity": {
    "P0": 0,
    "P1": 1,
    "P2": 2
  },
  "openFindingsCount": 3,
  "resolvedFindingsCount": 1,
  "convergenceScore": 0.44,
  "graphConvergenceScore": 1.01,
  "graphDecision": "STOP_ALLOWED",
  "graphBlockers": [],
  "corruptionWarnings": []
}
```

This file is machine-owned and must stay synchronized with the latest iteration delta, dashboard metrics, and synthesized review report.

### Blocked-Stop and Corruption-Tracking Additions

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `blockedStopHistory` | array | One entry per `blocked_stop` JSONL event: `{run, blockedBy, gateResults, recoveryStrategy, timestamp}`. Rendered in the dashboard `BLOCKED STOPS` section and can drive the strategy `next-focus` anchor when blocked-stop is the most recent loop event. |
| `persistentSameSeverity` | array | Findings observed in ≥2 iterations with NO severity transitions beyond initial discovery. REQ-018 split of the deprecated `repeatedFindings` bucket. |
| `severityChanged` | array | Findings that went through at least one severity transition (P0↔P1↔P2) in their `transitions` history. |
| `repeatedFindings` | array | **Deprecated.** Union of `persistentSameSeverity` and `severityChanged`. Retained for backward compatibility, new code should read the split arrays. |
| `corruptionWarnings` | array | Per-line corruption reports from `parseJsonlDetailed()`: `{line, raw, error}`. Non-empty means the reducer detected malformed JSONL. |

### Default Values

When no `graph_convergence` event has been recorded yet, defaults are `graphConvergenceScore: 0`, `graphDecision: null`, and `graphBlockers: []`.
When no `blocked_stop` event has been recorded yet, `blockedStopHistory: []`.
When JSONL parses cleanly, `corruptionWarnings: []`.

### Fail-Closed Semantics (REQ-015, REQ-016)

- **Malformed JSONL**: The reducer CLI exits with code `2` and writes a warning to stderr when `corruptionWarnings.length > 0`. Pass `--lenient` (or `lenient:true` to `reduceReviewState`) to escape-hatch out and preserve the v1.2.0.0 fail-open behavior for legacy packets.
- **Missing machine-owned anchors**: `replaceAnchorSection()` throws `Error('Missing machine-owned anchor "<id>" in deep-review strategy file.')` when the strategy file is present but lacks one of the required anchors. Pass `--create-missing-anchors` (or `createMissingAnchors:true`) to bootstrap empty strategy files by appending the missing anchor blocks.
- **Dashboard surfaces**: `CORRUPTION WARNINGS` section lists detected lines. `BLOCKED STOPS` section lists `blockedStopHistory` entries. `GRAPH CONVERGENCE` section reports `graphConvergenceScore` / `graphDecision` / `graphBlockers`.
- **Strategy next-focus override**: When the latest `blocked_stop` event timestamp is newer than the latest iteration timestamp, the reducer rewrites the strategy `next-focus` anchor to surface the blocking gates and recovery hint so operators see the blocker before choosing the next iteration direction.

---

## 6. ITERATION FILES (review/iterations/iteration-NNN.md)

Write-once files. One per iteration, zero-padded 3-digit naming.

### Structure

```markdown
# Iteration [N]: [Focus Area]

## Focus
[Dimension(s), files, scope investigated]

## Scorecard
- Dimensions covered: [list]
- Files reviewed: [count]
- New findings: P0=[n] P1=[n] P2=[n]
- Refined findings: P0=[n] P1=[n] P2=[n]
- New findings ratio: [0.0-1.0]

## Findings

### P0, Blocker
- **F[NNN]**: [Title], `file:line`, [Description with evidence]

### P1, Required
- **F[NNN]**: [Title], `file:line`, [Description with evidence]

### P2, Suggestion
- **F[NNN]**: [Title], `file:line`, [Description with evidence]

## Cross-Reference Results
| Protocol | Status | Gate | Evidence | Notes |
|----------|--------|------|----------|-------|
| spec_code | pass/partial/fail | hard | file:line | ... |

## Assessment
- New findings ratio: [0.0-1.0]
- Dimensions addressed: [list]
- Novelty justification: [breakdown]

## Ruled Out
- [Approach]: [Why], [Evidence]

## Dead Ends
- [Direction]: [Why]

## Recommended Next Focus
[What to investigate next]
```

Every finding must include: unique ID (`F001`...), severity (`P0`/`P1`/`P2`), concrete `file:line` evidence, and dimension tag.

---

## 7. REVIEW REPORT (review/review-report.md)

The review synthesis output contains 9 core sections plus a conditional `## Resource Map Coverage Gate` section when `resource_map_present` is true:

| # | Section | Description |
|---|---------|-------------|
| 1 | Executive Summary | Verdict, active P0/P1/P2 counts, scope, `hasAdvisories` flag |
| 2 | Planning Trigger | Why result routes to planning or changelog |
| 3 | Active Finding Registry | Deduped active findings with evidence, severity, dimension |
| 4 | Remediation Workstreams | Ordered lanes grouped by dependency or area |
| 5 | Spec Seed | Minimal spec updates implied by findings |
| 6 | Plan Seed | Initial remediation tasks from findings |
| 7 | Traceability Status | Core vs overlay protocol outcomes and gaps |
| 8 | Resource Map Coverage Gate | Present only when `{spec_folder}/resource-map.md` existed at init, contains touched entries, untouched entries (`expected-by-scope` vs `gap`), and implementation paths absent from the map |
| 9 | Deferred Items | Advisory findings, blocked items, follow-up checks |
| 10 | Audit Appendix | Coverage, convergence replay, audit detail |

**Executive Summary** includes verdict (`PASS`/`CONDITIONAL`/`FAIL`), active finding counts, `hasAdvisories` boolean (PASS + P2 > 0), scope description, and convergence reason.

**Planning Trigger** routes FAIL/CONDITIONAL to remediation planning, PASS to changelog creation.

**Active Finding Registry** lists each active finding with findingId, severity, dimension, title, file:line evidence, first/last seen iteration, and status.

**Remediation Workstreams** group related findings into ordered lanes with constituent finding IDs and execution order.

**Spec Seed / Plan Seed** provide minimal spec updates and initial remediation tasks referencing finding IDs and target files.

**Traceability Status** reports per-protocol pass/partial/fail with gating class and evidence.

**Resource Map Coverage Gate** is emitted only when `resource_map_present` is true and MUST contain exactly three bullets: touched entries, untouched entries (`expected-by-scope` vs `gap`), and implementation paths absent from the map.

**Deferred Items** captures advisory findings, blocked protocols, and future follow-up checks.

**Audit Appendix** contains iteration table, convergence signal replay, file coverage matrix, and dimension breakdown.

---

## 8. DASHBOARD (review/deep-review-dashboard.md)

Auto-generated summary. Never manually edited.

- **Path**: `{artifact_dir}/deep-review-dashboard.md`
- **Generated from**: JSONL state log + strategy + findings registry
- **Refresh**: Regenerated after every iteration
- **Protection**: `auto-generated` in fileProtection

### Sections

| Section | Source | Description |
|---------|--------|-------------|
| Findings Summary | JSONL | Active P0/P1/P2 counts, new vs refined |
| Progress Table | JSONL | Run, status, focus, dimensions, ratio, duration |
| Coverage | Strategy + JSONL | Dimension completion, file coverage, protocol status |
| Trend | JSONL signals | Rolling average, composite stop score, trajectory |

**Rules:** Sole inputs are JSONL + strategy. Overwrite entirely on refresh. Read-only for all agents.

1: ---
2: title: Deep Review Loop Protocol
3: description: Complete lifecycle specification for the autonomous deep review loop, initialization, iteration, convergence, synthesis, and save phases.
4: trigger_phrases:
5:   - "deep review loop protocol"

14: 
15: # Deep Review Loop Protocol
16: 
17: Complete lifecycle specification for the autonomous deep review loop.
18: 
19: ---
20: 
21: ## 1. OVERVIEW
22: 
23: ### Purpose
24: 
25: The deep review loop has 4 phases: initialization, iteration (repeated), synthesis, and save. The YAML workflow manages the lifecycle. The `@deep-review` agent (LEAF-only, no WebFetch) executes individual review iterations with fresh context each time.
26: 
27: ### When to Use
28: 
29: - Initiating a deep-review loop on a spec folder, skill, agent, or track.
30: - Resuming a paused or interrupted review session from prior state.
31: - Validating the 4-phase lifecycle and lineage protocol against current behavior.
32: - Auditing iteration-dispatch, convergence, or save-phase contracts before changes.
33: 
34: ```
35: ┌──────────────┐     ┌───────────────────────────────┐     ┌───────────────┐     ┌──────────┐
36: │  INIT        │────>│  LOOP                         │────>│  SYNTHESIS    │────>│  SAVE    │
37: │              │     │  ┌─────────────────────────┐  │     │               │     │          │
38: │ Scope        │     │  │ Read State              │  │     │ Finding       │     │ Memory   │
39: │ Discovery    │     │  │ Check Convergence       │  │     │ Registry      │     │ Context  │
40: │ Dimension    │     │  │ Check Pause Sentinel    │  │     │ Dedup         │     │ Save     │
41: │ Ordering     │     │  │ Generate State Summary  │  │     │               │     │          │
42: │ Traceability │     │  │ Dispatch @deep-review   │  │     │ Severity      │     │          │
43: │ Planning     │     │  │ Evaluate Results        │  │     │ Reconcile     │     │          │
44: │ State Files  │     │  │ Claim Adjudication      │  │     │               │     │          │
45: │              │     │  │ Dashboard Generation    │  │     │ Replay        │     │          │
46: │              │     │  │ Loop Decision           │  │     │ Validation    │     │          │
47: │              │     │  └────────┬────────────────┘  │     │               │     │          │
48: │              │     │           │ repeat            │     │ review-report │     │          │
49: │              │     │           │                   │     │ (9 core +     │     │          │
50: │              │     │           │                   │     │  resource-map)│     │          │
51: └──────────────┘     └───────────────────────────────┘     └───────────────┘     └──────────┘
52: ```
53: 
54: ### Core Innovation
55: 
56: Each agent dispatch gets a fresh context window. State continuity comes from files on disk, not in-context memory. This eliminates context degradation in long review sessions where accumulated findings would otherwise bias subsequent dimensions.
57: 
58: ### Key References
59: 
60: | Resource | Path | Purpose |
61: |----------|------|---------|
62: | Review contract | `assets/review_mode_contract.yaml` | Dimensions, verdicts, gates, protocols |
63: | Auto workflow | `.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_review_auto.yaml` | Unattended review loop |
64: | Confirm workflow | `.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_review_confirm.yaml` | Step-by-step review with approval gates |
65: | Agent | `@deep-review` (LEAF) | Single iteration executor, no sub-agents, no WebFetch |
66: | Memory save | `generate-context.js` | Context preservation script |
67: 
68: ---
69: 
70: ## 2. PHASE 1: INITIALIZATION
71: 
72: ### Purpose
73: 
74: Set up all state files for a new review session. Discover the scope, order dimensions by risk priority, establish the traceability protocol plan, and create the review state packet.
75: 
76: ### Steps
77: 
78: 1. **Classify session state before writing**:
79:    - `fresh`: no config/state/strategy files exist
80:    - `resume`: config + state + strategy all exist and agree
81:    - `completed-session`: consistent prior state with `config.status == "complete"`
82:    - `invalid-state`: partial or contradictory artifacts
83: 
84: 2. **Resolve local artifact owner**: `artifact_dir = resolveArtifactRoot(specFolder, 'review').artifactDir`, then `mkdir -p {artifact_dir}/iterations`
85: 
86: 3. **Scope discovery**: Resolve the review target into a concrete file list using one of 5 target types:
87: 
88:    | Target Type | CLI Value | Resolution Strategy |
89:    |-------------|-----------|---------------------|
90:    | **Spec Folder** | `spec-folder` | Read `spec.md`, `plan.md`, implementation files listed in `tasks.md` |
91:    | **Skill** | `skill` | Read `SKILL.md`, `references/`, `assets/`, `scripts/`, find agent definitions and command entry points |
92:    | **Agent** | `agent` | Find agent definitions across all runtimes, compare for consistency |
93:    | **Track** | `track` | List all child spec folders under the track, read `spec.md` + `checklist.md` for each |
94:    | **Files** | `files` | Expand glob patterns, validate existence, discover cross-references |
95: 
96:    Store the resolved file list in strategy.md "Files Under Review".
97: 
98: 4. **Dimension ordering**: Order the 4 review dimensions for iteration based on risk priority:
99: 
100:    | Priority | Dimension | Rationale |
101:    |----------|-----------|-----------|
102:    | 1 | Correctness | Logic errors have the highest blast radius |
103:    | 2 | Security | Vulnerabilities require immediate attention |
104:    | 3 | Traceability | Spec/code alignment ensures completeness |
105:    | 4 | Maintainability | Patterns and documentation quality (lowest immediate risk) |
106: 
107:    Default order: D1 Correctness, D2 Security, D3 Traceability, D4 Maintainability. This order is configurable but the default prioritizes highest-impact dimensions first.
108: 
109: 5. **Traceability protocol planning**: Partition the 6 cross-reference protocols into core vs overlay, and schedule only applicable overlays for the target type:
110: 
111:    | Level | Protocol | Applies To | Gate Class | Purpose |
112:    |-------|----------|------------|------------|---------|
113:    | Core | `spec_code` | all targets | hard | Verify normative claims resolve to shipped behavior |
114:    | Core | `checklist_evidence` | all targets | hard | Verify checked completion claims have evidence |
115:    | Overlay | `skill_agent` | skill | advisory | Verify SKILL.md and runtime agents agree |
116:    | Overlay | `agent_cross_runtime` | agent | advisory | Verify runtime agent parity |
117:    | Overlay | `feature_catalog_code` | skill, spec-folder, track, files | advisory | Verify catalog claims match capability |
118:    | Overlay | `playbook_capability` | skill, agent, spec-folder | advisory | Verify playbook scenarios match executable reality |
119: 
120:    Only schedule overlay protocols that apply to the resolved target type.
121: 
122: 6. **Write config**: `{artifact_dir}/deep-review-config.json` with `mode: "review"`, lineage metadata (`sessionId`, `parentSessionId`, `lineageMode`, `generation`, `continuedFromRun`, `releaseReadinessState`), and review-specific fields including target, target type, dimensions, protocol plan, and release-readiness state.
123: 
124: 7. **Initialize state log**: First line of `{artifact_dir}/deep-review-state.jsonl` with config record including `mode: "review"` and the lineage fields.
125: 
126: 8. **Initialize reducer state**: Create `{artifact_dir}/deep-review-findings-registry.json` with empty `openFindings`, `resolvedFindings`, `repeatedFindings`, `dimensionCoverage`, `findingsBySeverity`, and `convergenceScore`.
127: 
128: 9. **Initialize strategy**: `{artifact_dir}/deep-review-strategy.md` from review template with:
129:    - Topic (review target description)
130:    - Review Dimensions checklist
131:    - Files Under Review table
132:    - Cross-Reference Status table grouped by core vs overlay
133:    - Known Context from `memory_context()` results (if any)
134:    - Resource-map snapshot when `{spec_folder}/resource-map.md` exists at init
135:    - `resource-map.md not present. Skipping coverage gate` when it does not
136:    - Review Boundaries from config
137: 9a. **Initialize resource-map coverage state**:
138:    - If `{spec_folder}/resource-map.md` exists at init: set `resource_map_present = true`, add `Resource Map Coverage` to the Review Charter, and persist a concise map summary in `Known Context`
139:    - If absent: set `resource_map_present = false`, record the skip note in `Known Context`, and continue without failing
140: 
141: 10. **Validate review charter**:
142:    - Verify strategy.md contains Non-Goals and Stop Conditions sections (may be empty but must exist)
143:    - In **confirm mode**: present the charter (target, dimensions, scope, non-goals) for user review before proceeding
144:    - In **auto mode**: accept automatically and continue
145: 
146: 11. **Resume only if config, JSONL, strategy, and findings registry agree**. Otherwise halt for repair instead of guessing.
147: 
148: ### Outputs
149: 
150: | File | Location | Purpose |
151: |------|----------|---------|
152: | Config | `{artifact_dir}/deep-review-config.json` | Review parameters (immutable after init) |
153: | State | `{artifact_dir}/deep-review-state.jsonl` | Iteration log (1 initial line) |
154: | Registry | `{artifact_dir}/deep-review-findings-registry.json` | Reducer-owned findings state |
155: | Strategy | `{artifact_dir}/deep-review-strategy.md` | Dimensions, findings, next focus |
156: 
157: ---
158: 
159: ## 3. PHASE 2: ITERATION LOOP
160: 
161: ### Loop Steps (repeated until convergence)
162: 
163: #### Step 1: Read State
164: 
165: - Read `deep-review-state.jsonl` -- count iterations, extract `newFindingsRatio`, `findingsSummary`, `findingsNew`, `traceabilityChecks`, and lineage data
166: - Read `deep-review-findings-registry.json` -- extract `dimensionsCovered`, `findingsBySeverity`, `openFindings`, `resolvedFindings`, and `convergenceScore`
167: - Read `{artifact_dir}/deep-review-strategy.md` -- get next focus dimension/files, remaining dimensions, and protocol gaps
168: 
169: Reducer contract for every loop refresh:
170: 
171: | Reducer Part | Canonical Value | Notes |
172: |--------------|-----------------|-------|
173: | Inputs | `fullJSONLHistory`, `iterationFiles`, `priorReducedState` | Normal loop refresh is full-history: replay the persisted JSONL state and current iteration corpus each refresh so reducer output stays deterministic across restart, compaction, and synthesis audit paths. |
174: | Outputs | `findingsRegistry`, `dashboardMetrics`, `strategyUpdates` | The same refresh pass updates the canonical registry, refreshes dashboard metrics, and applies strategy updates. |
175: | Metrics | `dimensionsCovered`, `findingsBySeverity`, `openFindings`, `resolvedFindings`, `convergenceScore` | These metrics drive convergence decisions, dashboard summaries, and synthesis readiness. |
176: 
177: #### Step 2: Check Convergence
178: 
179: Run `shouldContinue_review()` (see `../deep-research/references/convergence/convergence.md` Section 10.3):
180: 
181: - Max iterations reached? `STOP`
182: - Stuck count `>= 2` using `noProgressThreshold = 0.05`? `STUCK_RECOVERY`
183: - Composite convergence votes `STOP` only after:
184:   - Rolling average uses `rollingStopThreshold = 0.08`
185:   - Dimension coverage reaches 100% across the 4-dimension model
186:   - Coverage has aged through `minStabilizationPasses >= 1`
187:   - Required traceability protocols are covered
188:   - Evidence, scope, and coverage gates pass
189: - Otherwise: `CONTINUE`
190: 
191: If convergence math or a hard-stop candidate points to STOP, the workflow must run the review legal-stop decision tree before actually stopping. That decision tree records nine review-specific gates: `convergenceGate`, `dimensionCoverageGate`, `p0ResolutionGate`, `evidenceDensityGate`, `hotspotSaturationGate`, `claimAdjudicationGate`, `fixCompletenessReplayGate`, `candidateCoverageGate`, and `graphlessFallbackGate` (see `../convergence/convergence.md` §Section-1 blocked_stop event example for the authoritative shape). The Step 4a claim-adjudication path treats `claimAdjudicationGate` as a hard STOP gate, so the count covers both the convergence decision tree and the adjudication enforcement path. `candidateCoverageGate` and `graphlessFallbackGate` are v2-rollout gates that pass trivially when the review-depth-v2 search path is inactive. If any gate fails, the loop does **not** stop. Instead it emits a first-class `blocked_stop` JSONL event with:
192: 
193: - `blockedBy`: array of the failed review gate names
194: - `gateResults`: per-gate pass/fail payloads using the review gate names above
195: - `recoveryStrategy`: one-line hint describing the next review action
196: 
197: The blocked-stop event is append-only evidence that legal-stop blocked the run. The loop then continues with the recovery or next-focus path rather than synthesizing.
198: 
199: Convergence signals and weights:
200: 
201: | Signal | Weight | Description |
202: |--------|--------|-------------|
203: | Rolling Average | 0.30 | Last 2 severity-weighted `newFindingsRatio` values average below `rollingStopThreshold` |
204: | MAD Noise Floor | 0.25 | Latest ratio within noise floor derived from historical ratios |
205: | Dimension Coverage | 0.45 | All 4 dimensions plus required traceability protocols covered, with stabilization |
206: 
207: **P0 override**: Any new P0 finding sets `newFindingsRatio >= 0.50`, blocking convergence regardless of composite score.
208: 
209: #### Step 2a: Check Pause Sentinel
210: 
211: Before dispatching, check for a pause sentinel file:
212: 
213: 1. Check if `review/.deep-review-pause` exists (note: the file name uses the shared `-pause` suffix)
214: 2. If present:
215:    - Log event to JSONL: `{"type":"event","event":"userPaused","mode":"review","stopReason":"userPaused","reason":"sentinel file detected"}`
216:    - Halt the loop with message:
217:      ```
218:      Review paused. Delete review/.deep-review-pause to resume.
219:      Current state: Iteration {N}, {reviewed}/{total} dimensions complete, {P0}/{P1}/{P2} findings.
220:      ```
221:    - Do NOT exit to synthesis -- the loop is suspended, not stopped
222: 3. On resume (file deleted and loop restarted):
223:    - Log event: `{"type":"event","event":"resumed","fromIteration":N}`
224:    - Continue from step_read_state
225: 
226: **Use case**: In autonomous mode, this provides the only graceful intervention mechanism short of killing the process. Users can create the sentinel file at any time to pause review between iterations.
227: 
228: Normalization rule: if the runtime first observes a raw `paused` condition or a raw `stuck_recovery` condition, it MUST rewrite the emitted JSONL event names to `userPaused` and `stuckRecovery` before appending them. Persisted review JSONL should never contain raw `paused` or raw `stuck_recovery` rows after emission.
229: 
230: #### Step 2b: Generate State Summary
231: 
232: Generate a compact state summary (~200 tokens) for injection into the dispatch prompt:
233: 
234: ```
235: STATE SUMMARY (auto-generated, review mode):
236: Iteration: {N} of {max} | Mode: review
237: Target: {config.reviewTarget} ({config.reviewTargetType})
238: Dimensions: {reviewed}/{total} complete | Next: {nextDimension}
239: Findings: P0:{count} P1:{count} P2:{count} active
240: Traceability: core={core_status} overlay={overlay_status}
241: Last 2 ratios: {ratio_N-1} -> {ratio_N} | Stuck count: {stuck_count}
242: Provisional verdict: {PASS|CONDITIONAL|FAIL|PENDING} | hasAdvisories={hasAdvisories}
243: Next focus: {strategy.nextFocus}
244: ```
245: 
246: This summary is prepended to the dispatch context (Step 3) to ensure the agent has baseline context even if detailed strategy.md reading fails or is incomplete.
247: 
248: #### Step 3: Dispatch Agent
249: 
250: Dispatch `@deep-review` with review-specific context:
251: 
252: ```
253: {state_summary}  // Auto-generated (Step 2b)
254: 
255: Review Target: {config.reviewTarget}
256: Review Mode: {config.reviewTargetType}
257: Iteration: {N} of {maxIterations}
258: Focus Dimension: {strategy.nextFocus.dimension}
259: Focus Files: {strategy.nextFocus.files}
260: Remaining Dimensions: {strategy.remainingDimensions}
261: Traceability Protocols:
262:   - Core: {core_protocols}
263:   - Overlay: {overlay_protocols}
264: Active Findings: {findingsSummary}
265: State Files:
266:   - Config: {artifact_dir}/deep-review-config.json
267:   - State: {artifact_dir}/deep-review-state.jsonl
268:   - Registry: {artifact_dir}/deep-review-findings-registry.json
269:   - Strategy: {artifact_dir}/deep-review-strategy.md
270: Output: Write findings to {artifact_dir}/iterations/iteration-{NNN}.md
271: CONSTRAINT: LEAF agent -- do NOT dispatch sub-agents
272: CONSTRAINT: Target files are READ-ONLY -- never modify code under review
273: ```
274: 
275: #### Executor Resolution (spec 018 + 019)
276: 
277: Before dispatching, the YAML resolves the executor via `parseExecutorConfig` from `.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/lib/deep-loop/executor-config.ts`. The resolved `config.executor.kind` selects the dispatch branch:
278: 
279: - `native` (spec 018): dispatch `@deep-review` agent with model Opus.
280: - `cli-opencode` (spec 018): pipe rendered prompt via stdin to `opencode run --model X -c model_reasoning_effort=Y -c service_tier=Z -c approval_policy=never --sandbox workspace-write`.
281: - `cli-claude-code` (spec 019): `claude -p "$(cat prompt)" --model X --permission-mode acceptEdits --output-format text` with optional `--effort Y`. Default permission-mode is `plan` (read-only). We override to `acceptEdits` so iteration writes succeed.
282: 
283: All branches share:
284: 1. Pre-dispatch prompt rendering via `renderPromptPack` (writes to `{artifact_dir}/prompts/iteration-{n}.md`).
285: 2. Post-dispatch validation via `validateIterationOutputs` (asserts iteration file + JSONL delta + required fields).
286: 3. Executor audit append via `appendExecutorAuditToLastRecord` (skipped when kind=='native').
287: 
288: Per-kind flag-compatibility is enforced at config parse time by `EXECUTOR_KIND_FLAG_SUPPORT` in `executor-config.ts`. Setting a flag that the chosen kind does not support throws `ExecutorConfigError` before dispatch.
289: 
290: Cross-CLI delegation (a running executor invoking other CLIs via its shell) is documented design intent. Runtime recursion detection is out of scope. See the SKILL.md Cross-CLI Delegation subsection.
291: 
292: Failure handling remains unchanged from spec 018: `schema_mismatch` → conflict event → 3 consecutive failures → `stuck_recovery`.
293: 
294: **Agent constraints**:
295: - `@deep-review` is LEAF-only: it cannot dispatch sub-agents
296: - No WebFetch: review is code-only and read-only
297: - Target 8-11 tool calls per iteration (max 12). Breadth over depth per cycle
298: - Tools available: Read, Grep, Glob, Edit (state files only), code_graph_query + Grep
299: 
300: #### Step 3a: Cross-Reference Protocol Execution
301: 
302: During iterations focused on the Traceability dimension, the agent executes applicable cross-reference protocols. Each protocol produces a structured result appended to the JSONL `traceabilityChecks.results[]` array.
303: 
304: | Protocol | Execution | Expected Output |
305: |----------|-----------|-----------------|
306: | `spec_code` | Compare normative claims in spec.md against shipped implementation | Pass/partial/fail per claim with file:line evidence |
307: | `checklist_evidence` | Verify every `[x]` mark in checklist.md has supporting evidence | Pass/partial/fail per checked item |
308: | `skill_agent` | Compare SKILL.md contracts against runtime agent definitions | Agreement/drift/disagreement per capability |
309: | `agent_cross_runtime` | Compare agent definitions across runtimes (.opencode, .claude, .opencode) | Parity/drift/divergence per runtime pair |
310: | `feature_catalog_code` | Compare catalog claims against discoverable implementation | Match/stale/missing per feature |
311: | `playbook_capability` | Validate playbook scenarios against executable reality | Executable/needs-update/impossible per scenario |
312: 
313: Each protocol result includes:
314: - `protocolId`: Protocol identifier
315: - `status`: `pass`, `partial`, or `fail`
316: - `gateClass`: `hard` or `advisory`
317: - `applicable`: Whether the protocol applies to this target type
318: - `counts`: `{ pass, partial, fail, skipped }`
319: - `evidence`: Array of file:line citations
320: - `findingRefs`: Array of finding IDs generated from this protocol
321: - `summary`: Human-readable summary text
322: 
323: #### Step 3b: Resource Map Coverage Audit
324: 
325: When `config.resource_map_present == true`, at least one loop iteration MUST audit implementation-vs-resource-map coverage:
326: 
327: 1. Read `{spec_folder}/resource-map.md`
328: 2. Read applied reports under `{spec_folder}/applied/T-*.md`
329: 3. Cross-check `target_files` from the applied reports against resource-map entries
330: 4. Classify outcomes into:
331:    - entries touched
332:    - entries not touched (`expected-by-scope` vs `gap`)
333:    - implementation paths absent from the map
334: 5. Emit any resulting findings with category `resource-map-coverage`
335: 
336: When `config.resource_map_present == false`, skip this audit and rely on the `Known Context` note: `resource-map.md not present. Skipping coverage gate`.
337: 
338: #### Step 4: Evaluate Results
339: 
340: After agent completes:
341: 
342: 1. Verify `{artifact_dir}/iterations/iteration-{NNN}.md` was created
343: 2. Verify JSONL was appended with review iteration fields:
344:    - `dimensions` (array of dimensions covered)
345:    - `filesReviewed` (array of file paths)
346:    - `findingsSummary` (cumulative P0/P1/P2 counts)
347:    - `findingsNew` (this iteration's new findings)
348:    - `traceabilityChecks` (protocol results if applicable)
349: 3. Verify strategy.md was updated (dimension progress, findings count, protocol status)
350: 4. Extract `newFindingsRatio` from JSONL record
351: 5. Update stuck tracking using `noProgressThreshold = 0.05`:
352:    - Skip if `status == "thought"` (no change)
353:    - Reset to 0 if `status == "insight"` (breakthrough counts as progress)
354:    - Increment if `newFindingsRatio < noProgressThreshold`
355:    - Reset otherwise
356: 
357: #### Step 4a: Claim Adjudication
358: 
359: Before the next convergence pass, the orchestrator adjudicates every new P0/P1 finding with a **typed claim-adjudication packet**. This step prevents false positives from inflating severity and distorting convergence, AND acts as a hard STOP gate: `step_post_iteration_claim_adjudication` appends a `claim_adjudication` event to `deep-review-state.jsonl`, and the next iteration's `step_check_convergence` legal-stop decision tree consults that event via `claimAdjudicationGate` (gate `f`). A missing or failing packet vetoes STOP even when every other gate passes.
360: 
361: Each new P0/P1 must carry a typed packet with the following required fields (see `../state/state_format.md` §9 for the full schema and a worked example):
362: 
363: | Field | Type | Description |
364: |-------|------|-------------|
365: | `findingId` | string | Matches the finding ID in the iteration body |
366: | `claim` | string | The single assertion the finding makes |
367: | `evidenceRefs` | string[] | `file:line` or `file:range` citations (≥ 1) |
368: | `counterevidenceSought` | string | Where the orchestrator looked for contradicting evidence |
369: | `alternativeExplanation` | string | An alternative explanation, even if rejected |
370: | `finalSeverity` | `"P0"` \| `"P1"` \| `"P2"` | Severity after adjudication |
371: | `confidence` | number `[0, 1]` | Orchestrator confidence in `finalSeverity` |
372: | `downgradeTrigger` | string | Concrete condition that would cause a future downgrade |
373: | `transitions` | object[] | Optional, required when `finalSeverity` differs from the originally asserted severity |
374: 
375: **Protocol**:
376: 
377: 1. Re-read the cited evidence at the referenced file:line locations.
378: 2. Seek counterevidence in adjacent code, docs, or prior iteration history.
379: 3. Record an alternative explanation even if it is rejected.
380: 4. Confirm or downgrade severity before the finding becomes convergence-visible.
381: 5. Emit the typed packet inside the iteration file so `step_post_iteration_claim_adjudication` can parse it.
382: 
383: **Failure semantics**: when any new P0/P1 finding is missing a packet or a required field, the workflow records `{"event":"claim_adjudication","passed":false,"missingPackets":[...]}` in `deep-review-state.jsonl`. On the next loop, `step_check_convergence` step 0 (universal pre-check) routes STOP to `BLOCKED` with `blockedBy: ["claimAdjudicationGate"]` until a follow-up iteration rewrites the packet. Downgraded findings have their `finalSeverity` updated. The original severity is preserved in the iteration file for audit trail.
384: 
385: #### Step 4b: Generate Dashboard
386: 
387: Generate `{artifact_dir}/deep-review-dashboard.md` with review-specific sections:
388: 
389: | Section | Content |
390: |---------|---------|
391: | Status | Provisional verdict (`PASS`/`CONDITIONAL`/`FAIL`/`PENDING`) and `hasAdvisories` flag |
392: | Findings Summary | P0/P1/P2 counts with deltas from previous iteration |
393: | Progress Table | Iteration number, focus dimension, `newFindingsRatio`, findings count, status |
394: | Coverage | Files reviewed, dimensions completed, traceability protocol status |
395: | Trend | Last 3 `newFindingsRatio` values with direction indicator (ascending/descending/flat) |
396: | Active Risks | Guard violations, stuck count, budget warnings |
397: 
398: Dashboard behavior:
399: - Auto-generated only -- never manually edited
400: - Overwritten each iteration (not appended)
